reasoning: ACLED protest counts generally follow a right-skewed distribution, meaning the historical mean (inflated by occasional large spikes) is typically higher than the median. As a result, the probability of any given future 30-day period naturally exceeding the 360-day historical average is slightly below 50%. Additionally, the absence of major national elections in late 2025 in Turkey dampens the likelihood of a predictable protest wave. However, following the instruction to increase uncertainty over the forecast horizon, the probabilities are pulled progressively closer to the 0.5 baseline for more distant resolution dates.
